identifies-deployable-units.evidence: Step 1 table identifies 5 components (Task Tracking, Time Logging, Reporting, User/Auth, PostgreSQL DB) all in the same Django monolith deployment unit.

produces-quantum-map.evidence: Step 4 table maps 1 quantum ('the only one') with all components, driving characteristics, and communication type.

analyzes-communication-patterns.evidence: Step 2 maps all communication paths as synchronous via Django ORM. 'Every single communication path is synchronous. All components talk through the Django ORM to the same PostgreSQL database.'

applies-3-criteria-definition.evidence: Step 4 applies all 3: '1. Deploy together? Yes ... 2. High functional cohesion? Yes ... 3. Synchronous connascence? Yes — all communication is synchronous via Django ORM and a shared PostgreSQL database.'

identifies-different-characteristics-per.evidence: Step 3 produces full characteristic table and correctly shows all are uniform. 'Every characteristic is uniform across all components.' This is the correct analysis for a monolith test case.

uses-connascence-for-boundaries.evidence: 'This is textbook synchronous connascence — all components share fate.' Uses shared DB as synchronous connascence evidence. 'A shared database means shared deployment.'

warns-uniform-characteristics.evidence: Step 3: 'Every characteristic is uniform across all components.' Correctly identifies the uniform case and warns against unnecessary distribution.

quantum-count-drives-style.evidence: Step 6: 'Quantum count: 1 ... Single quantum = monolith. With one quantum, there is no architectural justification for distribution.'
